history: The client recently ended a long-term relationship that began when she was 19, resulting in a divorce and ongoing financial difficulties. She is now a single parent to a young son and is navigating the process of settling child support with her ex-partner. She is also adjusting to new experiences, such as traveling alone with her son and visiting family without a partner. The report suggests she may have relied on her partner for emotional and financial support, and now faces self-doubt and vulnerability as she adapts to her new circumstances.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be abandoned
intermediate_belief: If I can't resolve financial issues with my ex-partner, it means I'm not capable of managing on my own and I'm letting my son down. I must be able to handle everything by myself, or it shows I'm inadequate. If others refuse to cooperate with me, it means I'm not worthy of respect or support.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I keep failing to resolve these problems, it proves that I'm not good enough and things will never get better. If I can't handle this on my own, it means I'm hopeless and my future will always be uncertain.
coping_strategies: She tends to withdraw from social interactions and avoids discussing financial matters when feeling overwhelmed. She may procrastinate on tasks related to her new responsibilities and seek reassurance from friends or family to cope with uncertainty and insecurity.
situation: Received an email from the ex-partner refusing to agree on child support and financial arrangements.
auto_thought: I can't believe he's refusing to help—I'm completely on my own now. I'm never going to be able to handle this or provide what my son needs.
behavior: She avoids responding to the email right away and puts off dealing with the financial paperwork. She withdraws from family or friends and spends time alone, feeling stuck and unsure what to do next.
